title: Conflict fault baseline scenario
description: Deterministic scenario skeleton for a representative context-conflict case where multiple bounded candidates may remain simultaneously admissible. This file is an integration asset and must remain aligned with canonical assets under common/. Conflict fault is distinct from insufficient context: information exists, but multiple candidates remain plausible and must not be selected arbitrarily.

steps.description: Publish a conflict-specific fixture intended to preserve multiple bounded candidate interpretations simultaneously through the registry-aligned operational context input plane.
Detect that more than one bounded candidate remains plausible. The system must not choose one candidate arbitrarily or dispatch an actuator command before confirmation.
Present bounded conflict-resolution candidates to the user/caregiver when appropriate, or move to safe deferral / caregiver confirmation if the conflict cannot be resolved safely.

If bounded conflict clarification is used, publish the clarification artifact to safe_deferral/clarification/interaction. This preserves the conflict cause and remains evidence-only, not authorization.

Observe the verification-safe audit stream and capture the conservative conflict-handling outcome, including the conflict cause, candidate set, confirmation requirement, clarification evidence if used, and final safe outcome.

notes: The exact conflict predicate must be derived dynamically from canonical policy and validator constraints rather than hardcoded here.
This skeleton is intended to exercise conservative resolution under ambiguity while preserving the conflict fault cause in audit records.
This scenario now uses a conflict-specific fixture instead of a generic Class 1 fixture.
Conflict fault is distinct from Class 2 insufficient context: the issue is not simply absence of information but simultaneous plausibility of multiple candidates.
Class 2-like clarification may be used to resolve the conflict, but candidate prompt text must not be treated as validator output or actuator authority.

Clarification interaction evidence must use safe_deferral/clarification/interaction when published and must not be interpreted as validator approval, actuation command, emergency trigger authority, or doorlock authorization.
If the conflict cannot be resolved with user/caregiver confirmation, the safe outcome is safe deferral or caregiver confirmation.
Class 1 autonomous execution must remain limited to the canonical low-risk lighting catalog when conflict is resolved safely.
